text.title: Problem Validation 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Problem Validation 2026 application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every evidence item MUST be ranked on the 5-level hierarchy (paid > committed > engaged > interested > stated); un-ranked items rejected.

rationale.source: Sean Ellis / Rob Fitzpatrick — Mom Test

rule.rationale: Behavioral commitments are costly signals; stated preferences are cheap.

rule.statement: Verdict 'validated' MUST require ≥3 tier-1 or tier-2 signals from cold (non-network) respondents.
rule.rationale: Warm contacts compliment to preserve relationship; cold strangers give unbiased signal.

rule.statement: Future-tense statements ("I would introduce you") MUST be classified as tier-4, never tier-2.
Validation MUST be re-run every 90 days; stale validations expire.
rule.rationale: AI-adjacent markets shift faster than annual validation can catch.

rule.statement: Evidence ledger MUST be authored and human-reviewed BEFORE the verdict is drafted in a separate pass.
rule.rationale: One-pass ledger+verdict anchors verdict on first evidence processed.
